highlights.text: I wrote on Wednesday at 4pm: “Bear Case Thursday: Begins on the failure of 6758”.
My general lean is bulls can try to backtest it.
Bears won the week in ES though, and after the “Bear Case” triggered on Thursday, bulls have been unable to recover that 6758-65 to take back control.
The first of these is what I call an “elevator down” sell.
After an elevator down sell, ES then squeezes back the other way, with the speed and ferocity of the squeeze proportionate to that of the sell.
The trigger for this is my core setup: The Failed Breakdown which occurs when price flushes a major low, traps shorts, then reverses.
We went elevator down off the futures open Sunday evening due to more bearish war headlines, selling down to 6588 from 6700+.
As always though, every elevator down sell in ES resolves in a squeeze, and Sunday evening was no different.
Thursday morning, price picked its path, and down we went ultimately into 6640 overnight last nightgoing elevator down .
As usual though, this would generate another Failed Breakdown and rip.
I wrote yesterday at 4pm: “The much safer entry is the Failed Breakdown of Monday’s 10:10AM 6641 low.

This is a huge low from which we ran into this weeks highs and the Failed Breakdown is obviously actionable.” Around 4:15AM last night ES lost 6641 by a point, recovered, and ripped around 100 points overnight.
In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll go over today’s Failed Breakdowns (these are key to know), and I’ll discuss the actionable plan for tomorrow.

post_title: Bears Win The Week For SPX. Can Bulls Comeback Monday? March 16 Plan
